item.dc:creator: BESTtaylorINAUSTRALIA

item.title: Thanks for alarming me, momma bun
item.description:
Noticed several blue spots in the snow around the perimeter of our yard. We live between some real shitty neighbors and a substance abuse recovery home, so sometimes sketchy things happen. It looked like something had dissolved into the snow, so I worried maybe it was some kind of rodent killer.
Did some Googling, and learned that when rabbits eat the bark of buckthorn, the phytochemicals that come out in their urine turn blue after being exposed to the sun! We have a big ol momma bun that hangs out around our yard year-round, and on closer inspection there is bunny poop around all the blue areas, so this is definitely her doing. Sure startled me, though!
Bun tax, not my photo because I haven’t managed to get one of her yet. Sauce that satisfied my curiosity: http://blog.adventurepublications.net/2020/02/blue-spots-in-the-snow-could-it-be-the-cottontail-rabbit/
item.dc:creator: Shloobs

item.title: Happy Birthday Imgur!
item.description: Getting in on the day with my 13th (unhidden) favorite! ? Absolutely loved this story when I first saw it. Hopefully it will be new to a few people. :3 Front page edit: I'm glad people are enjoying this story as much as I do! A few have mentioned that they recall seeing it in comic form and I found one that's pretty cool - https://www.demilked.com/monster-under-bed-comic-jwitless-art/ I don't know anything about the site, but the comic interpretation is really good!
item.dc:creator: SilverSatyr77

Interestingly enough, I was just chatting with someone about this the other day. Historically, when I received a compliment, I completely sabotaged any progress I made in life.
Cat tax: Cleveland sits on my bent knees, little dude will have a lay down too.
